1: A type: "My heart is not lifted up; my eyes are not raised too high." David's declaration of humility is the posture Christ embodied perfectly. Philippians 2:5-8 — Christ "did not count equality with God a thing to be grasped, but emptied himself, taking the form of a servant." Matthew 11:29 — "I am gentle and lowly in heart." The Son of God, who had every reason to lift his heart high, descended in humility — the type becomes the antitype without remainder.
2: A theme: "like a weaned child with its mother; like a weaned child is my soul within me." The weaned child (content in the mother's presence, no longer crying for milk) models the trust Christ calls his disciples to: Matthew 18:3 — "unless you turn and become like little children, you will never enter the kingdom of heaven." Matthew 11:28-30 — his yoke is easy; in him the soul finds rest without striving. The quiet soul is the soul resting in Christ.
3: A shadow: "O Israel, hope in the LORD from this time forth and forevermore." The eternal hope is "Christ in you, the hope of glory" (Colossians 1:27). Acts 28:20 — Paul was bound in chains because of "the hope of Israel" — which is Christ himself. 1 Peter 1:3 — "a living hope through the resurrection of Jesus Christ from the dead." The hope that spans "from this time forth and forevermore" is anchored in the resurrection and consummated at Christ's return.
